steps.title: Find a counterexample
steps.content: The student predicts that birds over 150 grams do not use deceptive defenses. To weaken that prediction, the data must show a bird above 150 grams that does use broken-wing display.

steps.title: Reject insufficient evidence
steps.content: Choice A says the pied-billed grebe does not use the display. Choice C lacks the key threshold comparison. Choice D describes birds under 150 grams, which does not contradict the prediction.

steps.title: Use the heavy displaying bird
steps.content: Choice B gives a direct counterexample: the pied-billed grebe weighs 409 grams and performs broken-wing display, despite being above the predicted threshold. The answer is B.
